title: Grassy Velvety Hangover-Cure Polish Pierogi
description: A jain-friendly Polish dish featuring unflavoured gelatin, brine-dried vegetables, and a grassy, velvety filling. Perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: Soak the unflavoured gelatin sheets in water for 5 minutes.
Mash the brine-dried cabbage and carrots together using a fork.
Chop the fresh parsley and dandelion greens into small pieces.
Sauté the garlic, parsley, and dandelion greens in vegan butter until fragrant.
Add the milled cabbage and carrots to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the soaked gelatin to the pan and stir until fully dissolved.
Add the vegan milk to the pan and stir until the mixture thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Spoon the mixture onto a clean surface and knead until smooth.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch.
Cut into circles using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
Place a spoonful of the filling in the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough over the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pierogi for 5 minutes.
Serve hot with your favourite topping.
